stories.headline: Achane's nursin' a busted wing -- but he's still gold
stories.read: Hold up, pardner -- De'Von Achane sat the team drills at Miami's minicamp, doin' nothin' but individual reps while that surgically-cleaned shoulder finishes healin'. Don't spook. The coaches say there's no danger to his trainin' camp in July, and a back who pulls the reins as often as Achane does is a top-shelf claim no matter how he practices in June. If a nervous leaguemate lets his price slip on a little offseason worry, you mosey right up and stake him. Just keep one eye on Jaylen Wright, the spare horse saddled behind him.

stories.headline: DJ Moore rides east to Buffalo -- she's about to gush
stories.read: Well now, the Bears shipped DJ Moore to Buffalo, and that's a vein worth pannin'. He's the clear-cut No. 1 catchin' from Josh Allen, with nobody else fightin' for the throws -- the saloon's talkin' a 25-percent cut of the targets, the richest situation of his whole career. That's a 1,000-yard season and a stack of touchdowns waitin' on a fella whose price ain't caught up yet. Reunited with Joe Brady too, the same boss who fed him a mountain of volume back in Carolina. Go stake that claim before the herd wakes up.

stories.headline: Malik Willis takes Miami's reins -- mind the empty saddlebags
stories.read: Straight off the wire: Malik Willis is the Dolphins' starter, signed for three years and $67.5 million to replace Tua. Now here's the rub -- Miami traded off both their star receivers, so this fella's ridin' into a season with mighty thin help and just six career starts to his name. His legs make him a fun late-round rental for the streamin' crowd, but bettin' your QB spot on him outright? That's fool's gold till we see who he's even throwin' to. Rent the horse for the right showdown -- don't buy the ranch.
